title: SEC Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill stops the Securities and Exchange Commission from making companies share information about climate change and the environment. Companies would only have to report climate information if it directly affects whether people should buy or sell their stock. The bill is meant to limit what environmental details companies must tell investors. It is called the Stop Environmental Calculations Act of 2025.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Financial Services.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Stop Environmental Calculations Act of two thousand twenty five or the SEC Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2  Prohibition on Requiring an Issuer to Make Climate-Related Disclosures That Are Not Material to Investors
sections.text: This section changes an existing law called the Securities Exchange Act of nineteen thirty four. It adds a new rule that says the Securities and Exchange Commission (the government agency that watches over the stock market) cannot force companies to share information about climate and environmental issues unless that information would help investors make important decisions about buying or selling stock in those companies.
